title: [epic] Extract Renode SX126x LoRa peripheral as standalone project
description:
Extract SX1262.cs as standalone Renode peripheral package.

WHY THIS DOESNT EXIST:
- Niche intersection: Renode users + LoRa users + CI-focused = tiny
- Hardware is cheap: Meshtastic devs buy 20 dollar boards, dont need sim
- Renode learning curve: C# + Renode API + SX126x datasheet = high bar
- No commercial pressure: LoRaWAN vendors ship hardware not simulators
- Community fragmentation: Meshtastic/RadioLib/LoRaWAN are separate ecosystems

WHAT WE HAVE:
- SX1262.cs (490 lines) - SPI opcodes, TX/RX, IRQ, FIFO
- Protocol bridge to external sim (TCP socket)
- Works with Zephyr sx126x driver

EXTRACTION SCOPE:
1. Standalone repo: renode-sx126x-peripheral
2. Pure Renode peripheral (no LICHEN dependency)
3. Two modes: loopback (single-node) and bridge (multi-node via external sim)
4. Example .repl snippets for common boards
5. CI with Renode test suite

TARGET USERS: Meshtastic devs, LoRaWAN stack devs, RadioLib users

ACCEPTANCE: Standalone MIT repo, works without LICHEN, README with quick-start
